title: Marbella Private Catamaran Charter Group Costs and Guide 2026
html:
For a private catamaran charter for a group in Marbella, you can expect to pay from EUR 2,500 for a full-day trip departing from Puerto Banús. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Marbella or list your own.

Where to set off in Marbella

Most private charters depart from Puerto Banús, the glamorous marina lined with superyachts and boutiques. Other departure points include Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo de Marbella), Cabopino Marina to the east, and the historic port of Estepona to the west. Further along the coast, the bay of Fuengirola and the beach at Elviria also offer anchorage stops. Many catamaran trips cruise towards the cliffs of Cabo de Plata or anchor off the island of Isla Verde for swimming. You can compare verified boat listings in a few minutes.

How much does it cost to rent a catamaran for a group in Marbella?

Private catamaran charters for a group of 12 guests start at around EUR 2,500 for a full day in high season. One option worth a look is the Princess F70 for hire.

To get a clear picture of available options, you can compare verified boat listings on BoatHire24. Prices vary by vessel size, crew, and included services such as catering and water toys. Half-day trips may run EUR 1,500, 2,000, while luxury catamarans with captains and hostesses can exceed EUR 4,000. Always confirm the exact inclusions with the operator.

Which boat to rent

Choose a boat that matches your group size and desired experience. Below are the most popular types for private charters around Marbella. If you own a boat, you can rent out your boat and earn from spare days.

  1. Catamaran (e.g., Lagoon 46), best for stable cruising with 12 guests; from EUR 2,500 full-day.
  2. Motor Yacht (e.g., Princess F70), best for speed and luxury with 10, 12 guests; from EUR 3,200 full-day.
  3. Sailing Yacht (e.g., Sun Odyssey 440), best for a classic experience with 8 guests; from EUR 1,800 full-day.
  4. RIB or Axopar 28, best for day trips and watersports with 8 guests; from EUR 800 half-day.
  5. Luxury Superyacht (e.g., Azimut 80), best for VIP service and long cruises with 14 guests; from EUR 6,500 full-day.

For those considering a specific motor yacht, the Princess F70 for hire is a popular choice, offering spacious decks and a flybridge ideal for socialising. See the full listing and availability on BoatHire24.

Cost breakdown by trip type

  • Shared sunset tour: EUR 80 per person for 3 hours, max 12 guests.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): EUR 600 for up to 8 guests.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): EUR 2,500 for 12 guests, including crew and basic snacks.
  • Luxury crewed yacht full-day: EUR 4,000, 8,000 for up to 14 guests, with gourmet catering and water toys.

Do you need a licence to rent a catamaran in Marbella?

For a private catamaran charter with a skipper, no licence is required; you simply relax and enjoy. To see how booking works, visit the boat search.

If you wish to rent a catamaran without a crew, you need an International Certificate of Competence (ICC) or equivalent, depending on your nationality. The operator will confirm the exact requirements at the time of booking. BoatHire24's listings include licence notes for each vessel.

A local tip: Book a catamaran with a stern platform and water slide for the best family experience in the calm waters of Cabopino Bay, especially in the morning when the breeze is light.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 1,500, 6,000+ full-day charter
  • Peak months: June to September (high season)
  • Typical trip length: 4 to 8 hours
  • Group capacity: 12, 14 guests on a catamaran
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

Can I rent a boat without a skipper in Marbella?

Yes, bareboat rentals are available if you hold a valid boating licence, such as an ICC or the Spanish PER (Patrón de Embarcaciones de Recreo), but many groups prefer a skippered charter for ease and local knowledge.

What is included in a private catamaran charter?

Typically, a crewed charter includes a skipper, fuel, insurance, and basic amenities such as towels and soft drinks; catering and water toys may be extra.

How far in advance should I book a catamaran in Marbella?

For peak season (June, September), booking at least two months ahead is recommended to secure your preferred vessel and date.

Can I bring my own food and drink on board?

Most operators allow you to bring your own provisions, but some offer catering packages; check the listing's policy before departure.

To find the perfect vessel for your group, use the boat search on BoatHire24 to filter by size, price, and availability. The platform handles the booking and provides verified listings so you can plan your day with confidence.

If you own a boat in Marbella, consider listing it on BoatHire24 to reach thousands of potential renters. You can rent out your boat and earn income when you are not using it.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
